other_observers: Andrew Spencer on 11/4; I'm the original finder.
specific_location: Chatfield State Park; Just east of the marina sandspit between the sandspit and the Plum Creek delta
description: A small "dainty" loon, body size a little larger than a Western Grebe's (Aechmorphorus occidentalis) body. Back and side dark brown. Sides and back of neck dirty gray. Front of neck dirty white with a dark "patch" just below the throat. Crown gray. Auriculars, cheek, chin and upper throat white. Eye dark reddish. Bill drab and dirty straw. Bill thin, relatively short (compared to other species of loon) with the lower mandible slightly angled upwards towards the tip. Head and bill held above horizontal. Crown rounded. No clear line of demarcation between gray of hindneck and lighter color of throat (like a Pacific Loon (Gavia pacifica) would have).Behaviors: Swimming and diving. I did not see this bird surface with anything in its bill. No interaction with nearby Western Grebes.Call: None notedPlumage: first year basic
photographer: Photographer: Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner
